title: node:test, Original Feed Fixtures, Property Cases, and Compile-Negative Evidence · Transfer Build

objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, entity, depth, date, identity, revision, malformed, and oversized fixtures with exact expectations.
Test URL, discovery, parser options, unknown validation, normalization, schedule, cursor, state, and policy with injected clocks and ports.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Original Fixture Matrix
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1501 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile original fixtures, deterministic async tests, compile-negative cases, and evidence non-claims; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, entity, depth, date, identity, revision, malformed, and oversized fixtures with exact expectations.
